sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Korea launches AI network alliance at MWC in 6G push
feed.summary: South Korea has formed a public-private AI network alliance (AINA) at MWC 2026 to secure leadership in AI-powered 6G networks and achieve 6G commercialization by 2030. The alliance, chaired by KT Corp. and including 34 companies like SK telecom and Samsung Electronics, aims to increase Korea's share of the global next-generation mobile communications market to 20 percent. This initiative comes amidst a global race to develop AI-driven telecom networks, with other major alliances also being formed by companies from the US, China, and various multinational corporations.

feed.title: Volume 40327.00 pre-market BKL.TO Invesco Senior Loan ETF (TSX) Mar 2026: watch
feed.summary: BKL.TO, the Invesco Senior Loan ETF, experienced a significant pre-market volume spike on March 6, 2026, with 40,327 shares traded at CAD 19.32, far exceeding its average volume. The ETF is trading above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, indicating a bullish structural signal. Meyka AI gives BKL.TO a "HOLD" grade with a one-year price target of CAD 26.30, suggesting a 36.15% upside.

feed.title: Halliburton Stock: Why Wall Street Is Watching This Oil Tech Giant Now
feed.summary: Halliburton is currently under the Wall Street spotlight due to converging factors like US energy demand, AI-driven drilling, and Middle East tensions. The oilfield services provider, which supplies essential tools and software for oil and gas extraction, is seen as a key indicator of US energy market movements. Investors are analyzing its strong exposure to North American shale and the Middle East, its embrace of digital optimization tools, and its commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson's Darzalex combination approved for bone marrow cancer
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson's Darzalex, developed in collaboration with Genmab, has received approval from the US FDA for treating recurrent or refractory bone marrow cancer. The approval applies to patients who have undergone at least one previous treatment. The article indicates that access to the full content requires a subscription.

feed.title: Southern Company Subsidiary Announces Key Leadership Appointment
feed.summary: Mississippi Power, a Southern Company subsidiary, has appointed Fredie Carmichael as Vice President of Customer Solutions and Corporate Communications, effective March 16. This internal promotion aims to strengthen the utility's leadership during significant technological and regulatory changes, leveraging Carmichael's extensive experience from Georgia Power. The appointment is crucial for ensuring service reliability and navigating the evolving regulatory landscape, which will be key performance indicators for investors.

feed.title: L3Harris Charts New Course with Leadership and Portfolio Overhaul
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies is undertaking a major strategic realignment, including executive team changes and the planned spin-off of its missile propulsion division through an IPO. This move aims to enhance the company's focus on defense capabilities and boost value in a high-growth sector. New financial leadership and the appointment of a head for the Missile Solutions segment are critical steps to prepare the unit for its future as an independent public entity.

feed.title: How Genuine Parts to split operations into Automotive, Industrial companies
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) will separate its operations into two independent, publicly traded entities, Global Automotive and Global Industrial, by the first quarter of 2027. This strategic move aims to unlock long-term shareholder value and enhance operational clarity, with each new company focusing on distinct market opportunities and investment strategies. The Global Automotive business will focus on the "do it for me" (DIFM) market, while Global Industrial will target consolidation in the fragmented industrial distribution landscape.

feed.title: Between Amphenol and Corning, Which Stock Looks Set to Break Out?
feed.summary: This article compares Amphenol (APH) and Corning (GLW) stocks, suggesting that Amphenol presents a stronger investment opportunity. Amphenol shows superior revenue growth, better profitability with higher operating margins, and a relatively lower valuation compared to Corning. The analysis highlights financial metrics and historical market performance to support its conclusion.

feed.title: First Solar, Inc. (FSLR) 2026 Guidance Disappoints Wall Street Firms, Triggers Downgrades
feed.summary: First Solar, Inc.'s (FSLR) 2026 guidance has disappointed Wall Street firms, leading to downgrades from analysts like Deutsche Bank and Jefferies. The solar company's projected 2026 revenue of $4.9–$5.2 billion fell short of analysts' estimates by 17%, despite reporting strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results. This has led some analysts to reassess the stock's potential, despite its leading position in the US photovoltaic solar industry.

feed.title: Deere & Company Stock: Navigating the Agricultural Downturn
feed.summary: Deere & Company is facing a challenging agricultural downturn due to declining commodity prices and high operational costs, with 2026 anticipated as the market trough for large machinery. The company is responding by pushing advanced technology, such as new tractor series and enhanced seeding systems, to drive future growth. Despite the market headwinds, Deere's diversification through its construction and forestry division and commitment to shareholder returns, including a quarterly dividend, are providing support, with the stock showing a significant year-to-date gain.

feed.title: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (NYSE:HLT) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: Twenty-four analysts have given Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (NYSE:HLT) a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average 12-month target price of $326.32. The company recently beat EPS estimates, reported a 10.9% year-over-year revenue increase, and issued strong FY2026 and Q1 2026 guidance. This positive sentiment comes despite a significant insider stock sale by CEO Christopher J. Nassetta.

feed.title: American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Given Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Analysts
feed.summary: American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) has received a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts, with an average 1-year price target of $135.10. The company recently exceeded Q4 EPS estimates and provided strong FY2026 guidance, alongside declaring a quarterly dividend of $0.95. Recent analyst upgrades and positive sentiment highlight the stock's potential, with institutional investors holding a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) Receives Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from 12 analysts, with an average one-year target price of $41.00. The company recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.285 (annualized $1.14), yielding approximately 3.0%, and reported Q4 FY2025 earnings that slightly missed estimates but set FY2026 EPS guidance of $1.90-$1.98. Major institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in the utility provider.

feed.title: Vector and Microchip strengthen collaboration to deliver pre‑integrated embedded solutions
feed.summary: Vector and Microchip have expanded their collaboration to offer pre-integrated hardware and software solutions for embedded control units, aiming to simplify development, reduce risk, and accelerate time-to-market. A key outcome is the immediate availability of MICROSAR IO support for Microchip’s dsPIC33A digital signal controllers. This partnership focuses on streamlining development cycles and supporting cost-sensitive sensor and actuator units in Software-Defined Vehicle architectures.

feed.title: How Johnson & Johnson Stock Gained 60%
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) stock surged 58% between June 2025 and March 2026, driven by strong revenue growth, improved net income margins, and increased investor confidence, particularly in its MedTech and oncology segments. This growth occurred despite patent expirations, thanks to innovative drug sales and significant FDA approvals. The company's 2026 revenue guidance of over $100 billion further boosted sentiment, though ongoing debate exists around its ability to offset challenges like Stelara's patent loss and litigation risks.

feed.title: Global Payments Inc. (NYSE:GPN) Receives Average Rating of "Hold" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (NYSE:GPN) has received an average "Hold" rating from twenty-four brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $91.52. The company recently reported strong Q4 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and provided positive FY2026 guidance. Institutional investors, including Norges Bank, have shown significant activity in GPN shares.

feed.title: Western Digital Insider Sells $2.5M in Shares
feed.summary: Cynthia Tregillis, an insider at Western Digital Corporation (NASDAQ:WDC), sold 9,324 shares of the company's stock for over $2.5 million. This transaction represents a 7.12% decrease in her ownership, potentially signaling a shift in sentiment or future prospects, which investors will likely monitor closely. The sale was disclosed in a regulatory filing, as required for insiders of publicly traded companies.

feed.title: UniFirst surges on report Cintas is nearing a purchase
feed.summary: UniFirst (UNF) stock surged after a report indicated that competitor Cintas (CTAS) is nearing a deal to acquire the company. UniFirst's market capitalization stands at $3.29 billion, while Cintas has a market value of $22.6 billion. Both companies operate in the uniform rental and facility services industry.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (NYSE:HPE) Receives Consensus Rating of "Hold" from Analysts
feed.summary: Analysts have given Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) a consensus "Hold" rating, based on reports from twenty-one firms, with a 1-year average price target of $25.29. The company recently reported mixed quarterly results, beating EPS estimates ($0.62 vs $0.59) but missing revenue expectations ($9.68B vs $9.91B). Insider selling has occurred, though institutional investors hold a significant 80.78% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Fifty-one brokerages have issued a "Moderate Buy" consensus recommendation for Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL), with an average 12-month price target of $366.57. The company recently reported strong earnings, exceeding expectations with $2.82 EPS and $113.83 billion in revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.21. However, the outlook is mixed due to positive momentum from Google Cloud AI wins and rising AI optimism, tempered by near-term challenges such as Play Store commission cuts, a wrongful-death suit related to Gemini, and regulatory scrutiny for Waymo.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Inc. (NASDAQ:ULTA) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. (NASDAQ:ULTA) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus recommendation from 28 brokerages, with an average one-year price target of $626.84. feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE:WFC) Given Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Analysts
feed.summary: Analysts have given Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE:WFC) a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average 1-year target price of $97.03. This positive sentiment follows the Federal Reserve ending a decade-long enforcement order, removing significant regulatory constraints on the company. Despite recent insider selling and a slight dip in stock performance due to broader market conditions, the regulatory relief is seen as a material structural positive for Wells Fargo's future growth and capital returns.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (NYSE:DAL) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Buy" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) has received a consensus "Buy" rating from twenty-four brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $80.38, despite recent stock pressure. The airline faces near-term challenges from geopolitical disruptions, rising oil costs, and an executive reshuffle, which have led to a 3.8% stock decline. Fundamentally, Delta beat Q4 EPS estimates and offers a modest dividend, although insider selling has been noted.

feed.title: ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW) Receives Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: ServiceNow, Inc. (NYSE:NOW) has received a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from 43 analysts, with an average 1-year price target of $192.06. The company recently beat quarterly earnings estimates, reporting $0.92 EPS on $3.57 billion in revenue, and its stock is trading near $120. Despite recent insider selling, institutional ownership stands at approximately 87%, and analysts expect ServiceNow to post 8.93 EPS for the current fiscal year.

feed.title: Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc. (NYSE:MAA) Receives Average Recommendation of "Hold" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Mid-America Apartment Communities, Inc. (NYSE:MAA) has received an average "Hold" recommendation from brokerages, with analysts setting a one-year average price target of $152.95. The company missed its quarterly EPS estimate and provided FY2026 guidance below analyst expectations, though revenue was largely in line. Despite increasing its quarterly dividend, concerns about sustainability arise due to a high dividend payout ratio of 161.9%.

feed.title: Teledyne Marine and M Subs sign MOU to accelerate maritime solutions
feed.summary: Teledyne Marine and M Subs have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to form a strategic partnership focused on accelerating maritime solutions for the UK Royal Navy and international naval markets. This collaboration aims to integrate their respective expertise in unmanned underwater vehicles, autonomy, and marine instrumentation to deliver next-generation solutions for modern maritime operations. Initial efforts include integrating Teledyne SeaBat sonars and deploying Slocum gliders and Osprey AUVs from M Subs' platforms.

feed.title: Federal Realty (FRT) Price Target Raised to $125 at Ladenburg amid Retail REIT Re-Rating
feed.summary: Ladenburg has raised its price target for Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) to $125 from $115, maintaining a Buy rating, citing a re-rating of the shopping center sector. The company reported strong Q4 2025 earnings with 6.4% FFO growth and provided guidance for approximately 6% growth in 2026. This positive outlook is supported by robust leasing activity, strategic acquisitions, and capital recycling, alongside plans for residential development to enhance long-term value.

feed.title: How will KC's data center boom impact electric bills? Evergy discusses new load rate for large customers
feed.summary: The article discusses how the proliferation of data centers in the Kansas City metro area will affect residents' electricity bills. Evergy has implemented a new large load rate plan for data centers, requiring them to pay higher rates, cover infrastructure upgrades, and face fines if they leave early, ensuring regular customers are not negatively impacted. Evergy believes this plan will ultimately lead to more reliable grids and lower costs for current customers.

feed.title: PTA Stock Fund Price and Chart — NYSE:PTA
feed.summary: This page provides an overview of the Cohen & Steers Tax-Advantaged Preferred Securities and Income Fund (PTA) listed on the NYSE. It includes current and historical price performance data, key statistics like dividend yield and market capitalization, and company information such as its sector, industry, and founding date. The article also touches on technical indicators and seasonal trends for the stock.

feed.title: Serve Robotics Showcases Growing Robot Fleet And Partnerships At Major Tech Events
feed.summary: Serve Robotics is promoting its autonomous delivery robots and AI platform at major tech conferences like SXSW and NVIDIA GTC to highlight industry acceptance and scaling commercial partnerships. These showcases provide investors with insight into the real-world operation and market positioning of Serve Robotics' technology, especially as the company aims to improve unit economics with a larger fleet and expanding city presence. Investors should monitor how these events translate into new city approvals, restaurant chain adoptions, and platform integrations, while also considering the company's current unprofitability and potential for shareholder dilution against its rapid revenue growth projections.

feed.title: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) Receives Average Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) has received an average "Moderate Buy" rating from 22 analysts, with a 1-year target price of approximately $115.95. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings estimates, reported $12.2 trillion in client assets, acquired Forge Global, and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.32. Despite positive growth indicators, the stock has experienced volatility due to significant insider selling and concerns regarding AI disruption, even though management is actively adopting AI.

feed.title: Insurer Humana prices $1B junior subordinated notes offering due 2056
feed.summary: Humana (NYSE:HUM) has priced a public offering of $1 billion aggregate principal amount of 6.250% junior subordinated notes due 2056. The offering is expected to close on March 13, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. The company intends to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of outstanding commercial paper.

feed.title: China approves Pfizer GLP-1 drug for weight loss
feed.summary: China has approved Pfizer's GLP-1 treatment Xianweiying for long-term weight management in overweight or obese adults, boosting competition in a market expected to be worth billions. Pfizer licensed the China commercialization rights for ecnoglutide from Sciwind Biosciences, which called the approval a breakthrough. This move solidifies Pfizer's position in the expanding weight-loss drug market, which already features significant sales from competitors like Novo Nordisk and Innovent Biologics.

feed.title: Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE:PRU) Receives Consensus Rating of "Hold" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE:PRU) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $115.00. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.40, resulting in an annualized dividend of $5.60 and a 5.6% yield. Notable insider buying activity, including a major shareholder acquiring 1.406 million shares, suggests increased insider ownership.

feed.title: Is Philip Morris International Stock Built to Withstand More Downside?
feed.summary: Philip Morris International (PM) stock recently dropped 5.2% due to concerns over slowing smoke-free product growth and regulatory hurdles. Despite strong fundamentals and an attractive valuation, the stock has historically underperformed the S&P 500 during economic downturns, taking longer to recover from crises like the 2018 correction. Investors are advised to consider a portfolio approach to mitigate individual stock downside risk.

feed.title: 11:11 | Iran warns US against ground invasion, Morgan Stanley cuts India, SoftBank seeks $40B loan
feed.summary: This article covers several key global and national news items, including Iran's warning to the US against a ground invasion following a joint strike, Morgan Stanley downgrading India's rating due to macro uncertainties, and SoftBank Group seeking a record $40 billion loan for its investment in OpenAI. Other topics include positive movements for Pine Labs and Mazagon Dock shares, the rupee gaining against the dollar, rising gold and silver prices due to geopolitical tensions, and demographic changes in Maharashtra and Andhra Pradesh.

feed.title: China approves Pfizer GLP-1 drug for weight management
feed.summary: China has approved Pfizer's GLP-1 treatment, Xianweiying, for long-term weight management in adults who are overweight or obese. This move intensifies competition in China's rapidly growing weight management market, which analysts predict will be worth billions. Pfizer licensed the commercialization rights for Xianweiying from Sciwind Biosciences, and the drug is already approved in China for Type II diabetes treatment.

feed.title: Is 21.1% Fall In First Solar (FSLR) Stock A Buying Opportunity?
feed.summary: The article poses a question about whether the 21.1% fall in First Solar (FSLR) stock presents a buying opportunity. However, the provided content is incomplete and primarily consists of website navigation, disclaimers, and an image, without any substantive analysis or financial data to answer the question. It appears to be an unfinished or placeholder article.

feed.title: eBay auctions are changing again: what US buyers must know
feed.summary: eBay is redefining its auction experience for US users, focusing on high-value categories, enhanced buyer protections, and AI-powered pricing tools. While auctions are no longer a default for everyday items, they remain a powerful strategic tool for collectibles, scarce tech, and liquidating bundles, offering a blend of psychology and data-driven tactics for both buyers and sellers to succeed in 2026.

feed.title: CarParts.com Inc (PRTS) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strategic Investments and Operational Efficiency Amid Revenue Challenges
feed.summary: CarParts.com Inc (PRTS) reported Q4 2025 earnings, highlighting strategic investments and improved operational efficiency despite a 10% year-over-year decline in net sales. The company achieved four consecutive quarters of improved contribution margin and adjusted EBITDA, driven by a premium partnership and cost restructuring efforts. PRTS aims to be free cash flow positive in 2026 through continued margin expansion and strategic partnerships.

feed.title: BofA Reiterates Buy on McKesson Corporation (MCK) March 2026
feed.summary: Bank of America, Leerink Partners, and Evercore ISI all maintained positive ratings on McKesson Corporation (MCK) on March 5, 2026, despite the announcement of the CFO's retirement. These reiterations suggest that analysts expect operational continuity and see the company's current strategy and fundamentals as intact. Investors should view these maintained ratings as a signal to review their position and the company's fundamentals, rather than an immediate trigger for aggressive buying.

feed.title: Phillips Jason D, Moody’s Corp chief acctg off, sells $157k in MCO
feed.summary: Jason D. Phillips, Chief Accounting Officer & Controller at Moody’s Corp (MCO), sold 333.123 shares of common stock for $157,437. This transaction comes as Moody's reports strong fourth-quarter earnings, though UBS and BMO Capital have lowered price targets due to valuation concerns, maintaining Neutral and Market Perform ratings respectively. Despite these adjustments, the company exhibits strong financial health and anticipates low-single-digit percentage growth in 2026, driven by robust debt issuance and outperforming its core ratings business.

feed.title: Adams Natural Resources Fund, Inc. (PEO) Leadership & Management Team Analysis
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the leadership and management team at Adams Natural Resources Fund, Inc. (PEO). It details the CEO's tenure and ownership, average tenures for the management team and board, and recent management updates. The report also includes Jim Haynie's compensation history relative to company earnings and lists key leadership and board members with their respective tenures and compensation.

feed.title: Sight Sciences Reports Preliminary Fourth Quarter 2025 Revenue Growth, Provides 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Sight Sciences, an eyecare technology company, reported preliminary unaudited financial highlights for Q4 2025, with total revenue reaching $20.4 million, a 7 percent increase from 2024. Full-year 2025 revenue was $77.4 million, a 3 percent decrease from 2024. The company anticipates 2026 revenue to be between $82 million and $88 million, reflecting 6 to 14 percent growth compared to 2025, driven by strong interventional glaucoma revenue and encouraging traction in interventional dry eye.

feed.title: Rocket Lab Stock’s Rebound Week: Fresh Launch, Hypersonic Test Calm Neutron Delay Fears A Bit
feed.summary: Rocket Lab's stock is rebounding after a week of successful launches, including a hypersonic test flight for the U.S. Defense Innovation Unit and a commercial satellite deployment. These achievements have helped assuage investor concerns over previous delays to the company's Neutron launch program. The company's potential role in U.S. defense efforts, especially in the context of international conflicts, is also positively influencing retail investor sentiment.

feed.title: Kempner Institute Announces 2026 Accelerator Awards Request for Proposals
feed.summary: The Kempner Institute at Harvard University has released its 2026 Accelerator Awards request for proposals, offering Harvard faculty access to its powerful AI Cluster. This award program supports research aligned with the Institute's mission to understand intelligence in natural and artificial systems, providing up to 64 GPUs for 30 days. Applications open on April 14, 2026, targeting projects that explore the foundations of intelligence or lead to new AI/ML methods and applications.

feed.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff's ICE jokes that upset employees and made company 'threaten' those who leaked them; make president Robin Washington tell employees at Townhall ...
feed.summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff's jokes about Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) tracking international employees caused internal turmoil and employee backlash. Company president Robin Washington addressed the controversy at an all-hands meeting, acknowledging employee concerns and stating that the company was "listening" and "adjusting." The jokes, which included a quip about ICE agents being in the building, also led to reports of Salesforce threatening those who leaked the incident to the press.

feed.title: Dr Reddy's Lab gets EIR from USFDA for Srikakulam facility
feed.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories has received an Establishment Inspection Report (EIR) from the USFDA for its Srikakulam formulations manufacturing facility. The inspection outcome was classified as Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), officially closing the inspection. This inspection included both a Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) review and a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI).

feed.title: Micron Bulls Are About To Meet Samsung's HBM4 Memory Chip (Rating Downgrade)
feed.summary: The author is downgrading Micron Technology to a "hold" rating ahead of its Q2 FY26 earnings print, citing Samsung's impending HBM4 production ramp as a significant downside catalyst. This could pressure Micron's market share and future guidance despite a forecasted strong memory cycle and constrained supply. While Micron has seen a substantial stock run, the current macro backdrop and competitive landscape necessitate consistent strong performance to maintain its valuation.
